WITH MostCommon AS (
    SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details
    FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
    GROUP BY behaviour_monitoring_details
    HAVING COUNT(*) = (
        SELECT MAX(cnt)
        FROM (
            SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
            FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
            GROUP BY behaviour_monitoring_details
        ) AS sub
    )
),
ExactlyThree AS (
    SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details
    FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
    GROUP BY behaviour_monitoring_details
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 3
),
StudentsMostCommon AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT student_id
    FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
    WHERE behaviour_monitoring_details IN (SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details FROM MostCommon)
),
StudentsExactlyThree AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT student_id
    FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
    WHERE behaviour_monitoring_details IN (SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details FROM ExactlyThree)
)
SELECT s.bio_data, s.student_details
FROM Students s
JOIN StudentsMostCommon mc ON s.student_id = mc.student_id
JOIN StudentsExactlyThree et ON s.student_id = et.student_id;

),
ThreeCount AS (
    SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details
    FROM Behaviour_Monitoring
    GROUP BY behaviour_monitoring_details
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT student_id) = 3
)
SELECT DISTINCT s.bio_data, s.student_details

    ON s.student_id = bm1.student_id
JOIN Behaviour_Monitoring bm2

  AND bm2.behaviour_monitoring_details IN (SELECT behaviour_monitoring_details FROM ThreeCount);

WITH RECURSIVE Parent_Collections AS (
    -- starting point: the collection named 'Nice'
    SELECT 
        c.Collection_ID,
        c.Parent_Collection_ID,
        c.Collection_Name
    FROM Collections c
    WHERE c.Collection_Name = 'Nice'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- recursive step: get the parent collection
    SELECT 
        p.Collection_ID,
        p.Parent_Collection_ID,
        p.Collection_Name
    FROM Collections p
    INNER JOIN Parent_Collections pc
        ON pc.Parent_Collection_ID = p.Collection_ID
)
SELECT Collection_Name
FROM Parent_Collections
WHERE Collection_Name <> 'Nice';

  ON linda.Fname = 'Linda' AND linda.LName = 'Smith'

    (dd.city1_code = linda.city_code AND dd.city2_code = s.city_code)
    OR

ORDER BY dd.distance DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH payment_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        invoice_id, 
        COUNT(*) AS num_payments
    FROM Payments
    GROUP BY invoice_id
),
max_count AS (
    SELECT MAX(num_payments) AS max_payments
    FROM payment_counts
)
SELECT 
    i.invoice_id, 
    i.invoice_status, 
    i.invoice_details
FROM Invoices i
JOIN payment_counts pc 
    ON i.invoice_id = pc.invoice_id
JOIN max_count mc 
    ON pc.num_payments = mc.max_payments;
